provenance.ruling_ref: scripts/stage2-retag/artifacts/completeness-screen.md (BORDERLINE Group A: six 'metadata card' bodies) + user verdict 2026-06-12 (supervisor session)
provenance.effect: These corpus records' stored content_text is a machine-written TAG-SUMMARY card (title + short description + lists of grades/themes/ingredients/skills), NOT lesson text — re-tagging it would read the old tags back circularly. The REAL lesson body was fetched READ-ONLY from each record's underlying live Google Doc (df@esynyc.org) and is supplied to export-corpus.ts as a sidecar at artifacts/body-overrides/<id>.txt (untracked, like artifacts/whos-who-body.txt). The export script substitutes the sidecar body for the stored content_text at export time. NO database write — the PROD content_text repair, if any, rides a later PR.
provenance.fetch_method: Long ids = native Google Docs (get_doc_content). Short ids = .docx Office files (get_drive_file_content). Bodies normalized via export-corpus.ts normalizeBody (strip \x0B, normalize \r\n/\r to \n).
provenance.fallback_note: Two other Group-A metadata-card records (Equivalent Ratios 1jfFP2nKtAti3HQZzX2Fi9X72M8BZ02uX, Mindful Eating 1SDsLLHlfBqIHSxvVVQrbOk96hlOkxOse) 404'd in Drive and could NOT be re-extracted; they are in data/corpus-exclusions.json instead (pre-authorized re-extraction fallback). The two Fattoush docs are a known near-duplicate pair — identical bodies are expected; both stay.
provenance.min_body_chars: export-corpus.ts enforces a per-override minimum-length sanity guard (MIN_OVERRIDE_BODY_CHARS) and refuses to run if a sidecar is missing or stub-sized, mirroring the Who's-Who guard.
